findings.description: The entire Typography Hierarchy table (font assignments per level for Print/Digital/Word fallback, e.g. Agenda vs Nunito Sans vs Aptos) and the available-weights list are not represented by any candidate rule. This is a major coverage gap in the cluster.

payload.description: Use Agenda for print headlines, Nunito Sans for digital headlines/body/footers (Title Case, bolder weights for digital headlines), and Aptos as the Word/PPT/Excel fallback across all levels; only approved weights per font (Agenda: Light/Regular/Medium/Semibold/Bold; Nunito Sans: Light/Regular/SemiBold/Bold/ExtraBold; Aptos: Regular/Bold) may be used.

findings.description: The [GENERAL]-tagged email font stack rule (CSS fallback stack, @font-face/Google Fonts loading requirement) is missing entirely from the cluster and its governance scope tag ([GENERAL]) is not captured anywhere.

payload.description: Email font stack must be 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif; Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported, relying on the stack for graceful fallback in Outlook and similar clients.

findings.description: The [GENERAL] email type scale (H1/H2/H3/body/secondary/footnote px and line-height specs, including the explicit 'brand-mandated minimum' 16px body rule) is not extracted as any rule, losing both the hard 16px minimum constraint and the general/email governance scope.

payload.description: Email type scale: H1 28/36, H2 22/30, H3 18/26, Body 16/24 (16px brand-mandated minimum for body copy), Secondary 14/21 (use sparingly, never primary), Footnote 12/18 (must look noticeably smaller and separated).

findings.description: Explicit hard prohibition 'Never set running text below 12px' (with ISI carve-out owned by locked component) is missing as a distinct must_not rule; it is not folded into any candidate rule.

payload.description: Never set running text below 12px, except ISI sizing which is owned by the locked component.

findings.description: This rule conflates two distinct normative statements from u_0019: (1) an unconditional 150% minimum line-height for all body copy, and (2) a conditional [GENERAL] rule requiring 160% line-height specifically for light text on dark backgrounds. These have different scope/conditions and should be split into two rules.

rule_a.description: Line height must be a minimum of 150% for all body copy (brand accessibility rule).

rule_b.description: For light text on dark backgrounds, line height must be increased further, e.g. to 160% (16px/26px).

findings.description: The rule only captures the second sentence of u_0020 (must_not two adjacent same size+weight blocks). The first, softer normative statement in the same unit ('Use a clear type scale / visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, last') is dropped and not represented as its own guidance rule.

payload.description: Use a clear type scale/visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, and last.
